demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: The skin over the bump on the back of his head looks rubbed raw, and there's clear watery fluid dripping out of it. He's only 2 months old. He's been crying with a really high-pitched cry for the last few hours and seems so upset — we don't know what to do.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: Parents noticed a small raw patch on the sac at the back of his head this afternoon with clear fluid leaking from it, and he has become more and more irritable over the last 4 hours.

symptom_narrative.duration: 4 hours and the fluid is still leaking
symptom_narrative.character: clear, thin, watery fluid dripping from a small raw spot at the top of the soft lump on the back of his head
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the crying is worse than his usual fussing — a high-pitched cry we haven't heard before
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: the midline lump at the back of his head, in the occipital area
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: the leaking and irritability have both been building over the afternoon
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: he settles a little when we hold him on his front, off the back of his head
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: he cries more when we lay him on his back
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: increasing irritability and inconsolable high-pitched crying; no jerking or fits that we've seen
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: the lump has been there since birth but it has never leaked or broken open before
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he is a fully dependent baby; we normally have to position him carefully so nothing presses on the lump

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: born with a soft lump at the back of his head (a swelling that was there from birth), waiting to see the surgeons about it
past_medical_history.how_managed: careful positioning at home to keep pressure off the lump; awaiting a neurosurgery appointment

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: his head is smaller than expected for his age, we were told
he has been slower than expected to reach his baby milestones
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: noted over the first couple of months

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 2-month-old infant cared for at home by both parents; both parents present and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: born with a midline swelling at the back of the head; smaller-than-expected head; slower than expected to reach early milestones

objective_data_offered.value: a soft midline lump at the back of the head covered by thin skin, with a small raw/broken area at the top of it, about the size of a fingertip
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: seen on a parent's phone camera; lighting and angle limited

objective_data_offered.value: clear watery fluid slowly dripping from the raw spot on the lump
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: the parents cannot tell what the fluid is; nature of the fluid is not verifiable over video

objective_data_offered.value: he is crying inconsolably with a high-pitched cry, most upset when lying on his back and calmer when held on his front
he feels about the same temperature as usual to us and is not floppy or unrousable at the moment
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parental impression only; no thermometer reading offered and infant temperature is unreliable by touch

objective_data_offered.value: he sleeps on a standard flat cot mattress without any special pressure-relieving pillow
